leadText: Play Rough (Japanese: じゃれつく Frolic) is a damage-dealing Fairy-type move introduced in Generation VI. It is: TM44 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR90 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM127 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM020 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A. In Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, it can be upgraded to the special Z-Move Let's Snuggle Forever by Mimikyu holding Mimikium Z.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.2 Generation IX 1.2.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Play Rough inflicts damage and has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Attack stat by one stage. May lower the target's offensive stats. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP The user plays rough with the target and attacks it. This may also lower the target's Attack stat. LA The user plays rough with the target and attacks it. This may also lower the target’s offensive stats. S V The user attacks by playing rough with the target. This may also lower the target's Attack stat. ZA The user attacks by playing rough. This may also lower targets' Attack stats.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD It damages an enemy. It could lower the enemy's Attack. * It damages an enemy. It could also lower the enemy's Attack. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Prior to Generation VIII , Play Rough was the only physical Fairy-type move, not counting Z-Moves or Max Moves . However, it is the only one that is widely distributed, as Spirit Break is exclusive to Grimmsnarl and Iron Valiant , while Magical Torque cannot be used by the player.
